name: kagome_b1_lanczos_refined

ansatz.comment: deliberately under-trained — Lanczos does the heavy lifting

sector_spectrum.interpretation: Lowest 4 eigenvalues of H restricted to (Γ, B_1) sector. E_2 and E_3 are degenerate (2-fold level). At this cluster size the 3 lowest globally also live in (Γ, B_1), so this matches the full-Hilbert k=3 result from test_kagome_lanczos_k_lowest. At larger N the spectra diverge.

notes: Lanczos post-processing on the sector-projected wavefunction reaches ED ground state to 11 digits in 33 Krylov iterations and 0.12 s, even from a deliberately under-trained ansatz with E_var ~ +1.0 (∼6.4 J above E_0). The B_1 projector guarantees nonzero overlap of psi_sym with the (Γ, B_1) ground state on the 4096-dim Hilbert space, and Lanczos converges to the lowest eigenvalue of any seed with nonzero overlap on it. P2.6 leverage move from architecture_v0.4.md realized end-to-end. Extended with sector-resolved low-energy spectrum via k-lowest Lanczos seeded from the same psi_sym; the Krylov subspace stays inside (Γ, B_1) because H commutes with the projector, so the returned k smallest Ritz values are the lowest k of H restricted to that sector — no sector-built sparse H needed.
